channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - remapradio.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for remapradio.com

item.title: This Week at Remap
item.content:encoded:
Apologies for letting the weekend newsletter get away from me. It was a tough weekend, because on Sunday, we dropped our nine-year-old away at camp for the week. No phones. No tablets. No watches. The only way to communicate with her is by sending a note through an app, at which point the camp prints out the notes and hands them over during lunch. She fell in love with the movie Parent Trap a year ago, and then started doing research on whether places like that actually existed. I'm not sure I would have been brave enough to be away from home for nearly a week at her age, but she's the one who pitched us, and I'm so excited for her. 😔

-pk, who is nearly done with Mina the Hollower

  • PODCAST: My Turn – Ocean's Eleven (feat: Rob, Chia, Janet) – Summer Game Fest meant I wasn't able to catch up on this movie ahead of the recording, but I'm already ahead of the game with Ocean's 12. Though, uh, well, hmm...I'm very curious to record that podcast when we get around to it lol
  • STREAM: Steam Next Fest (feat: Chia, Patrick) – It's that time again. Chia and I are going to download a bunch of demos and rocket through them!
  • PODCAST: Summer Game Fest Diary (Part Two) – You'll hear me walk through the many games I played at Summer Game Fest, the many interviews I conducted while playing those games, and closing thoughts on this year's show.
  • STREAM: TBD – We're still working out what this might be. There are so many Next Fest demos that it's a good chance we're doing even more of those.
  • PODCAST: Remap Radio – We'll see if I'm on the other side of Mina the Hollower by the time we record this, but Janet and I have so many other games to talk about from Summer Game Fest that we didn't get to last week.
  • STREAM: Blue Prince – The puzzle sluts are at it again, folks.
  • ARTICLE: Letter Series – This is probably happening, too. Topic TBD.

If you have any support issues, send details to support@remapradio.com

Patrick Klepek (he/him) is an editor at Remap. In another life, he worked on horror movie sets, but instead, he also runs Crossplay, a newsletter about parenting and video games. You can follow him on Twitter, Threads, Mastodon, and Bluesky.
